{{nihongo|'''Nomi Shrine'''|野見神社 (一宮市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 野見神社 |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町宮後|Q127432729}}字稲荷1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}}・{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月16日|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=野見神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''野見神社'''（のみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町|Q11376737}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}「野見神社」に比定され{{jalink|稲沢市|Q389562}}浅井の{{jalink|浅井神社 (稲沢市)||浅井神社}}に比定する説もある（{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}の『本国神名帳集説』や、それを受けた{{jalink|松平君山|Q6564909}}の『{{jalink|張州府志|Q137847623}}』（{{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}2年（1752年））、『{{jalink|尾張名所図絵||尾張名所図絵後編}}』（{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}12年（1842年））など）。、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}} * {{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} 上記3{{jalink|柱 (曖昧さ回避)|Q16283888|柱}}を{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100|主祭神}}に、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}と{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}する。 神社名の「野見」から、{{jalink|土師氏|Q10927758}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}である野見宿禰命、あるいは天穂日命と見るのが通説となっている。また、境内に{{jalink|今伊勢古墳群}}に含まれる'''野見神社古墳'''があり、{{jalink|勾玉|Q334232}}や{{jalink|埴輪|Q849376}}等が出土しているが、土師氏が{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}の製造のみならず、{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}の築造にも深く関わっていたことも参考になる。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明であるが、土師氏がこの地域を開拓したさいに創祀したと推測され、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では{{jalink|式内社#式内社の社格||国幣小社}}に列し、『国内神名帳|尾張国神名帳』では「従三位 野見天神」と記載されている。また、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}6年（{{jalink|1399年|Q6422}}）の「尾張国国衙正税未進注文」（『{{jalink|醍醐寺文書}}』）には「野見社」の名で見えているので、当時{{jalink|国衙領|Q11421344|国衙領内}}に{{jalink|社領}}を有する神社であったろうとの指摘もある『新編一宮市史 本文編上』、一宮市、昭和52年。。なお、大字名の「宮後（みやうしろ）」は当神社の北方に位置する村落の意味であろうとの説がある（{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}『尾張国地名考』）。 === 文化財 === * {{jalink|スダジイ|Q846994}} - 目通り2.5m高20m、同種の中でも稀な大木でありながら樹勢は旺盛であり、一宮市の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}に指定されている。 *野見神社古墳 - 長径約20m、墳高約1mの小規模な{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}で、{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前Ⅲ期（5世紀前半）の築造と見られている。なお、当古墳が含まれる伊勢古墳群は市の{{jalink|史跡|Q1081138}}に指定されている。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}{{jalink|今伊勢駅|Q6002985}}より徒歩15分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告 第8巻東海道3』、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、平成元年 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|野見神社|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]]

{{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町宮後字稲荷1929', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:野見神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646128}} == Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 野見神社 |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町宮後|Q127432729}}字稲荷1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}}・{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月16日|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=野見神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''野見神社'''（のみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町|Q11376737}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}「野見神社」に比定され{{jalink|稲沢市|Q389562}}浅井の{{jalink|浅井神社 (稲沢市)||浅井神社}}に比定する説もある（{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}の『本国神名帳集説』や、それを受けた{{jalink|松平君山|Q6564909}}の『{{jalink|張州府志|Q137847623}}』（{{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}2年（1752年））、『{{jalink|尾張名所図絵||尾張名所図絵後編}}』（{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}12年（1842年））など）。、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}} * {{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} 上記3{{jalink|柱 (曖昧さ回避)|Q16283888|柱}}を{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100|主祭神}}に、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}と{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}する。 神社名の「野見」から、{{jalink|土師氏|Q10927758}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}である野見宿禰命、あるいは天穂日命と見るのが通説となっている。また、境内に{{jalink|今伊勢古墳群}}に含まれる'''野見神社古墳'''があり、{{jalink|勾玉|Q334232}}や{{jalink|埴輪|Q849376}}等が出土しているが、土師氏が{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}の製造のみならず、{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}の築造にも深く関わっていたことも参考になる。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明であるが、土師氏がこの地域を開拓したさいに創祀したと推測され、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では{{jalink|式内社#式内社の社格||国幣小社}}に列し、『国内神名帳|尾張国神名帳』では「従三位 野見天神」と記載されている。また、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}6年（{{jalink|1399年|Q6422}}）の「尾張国国衙正税未進注文」（『{{jalink|醍醐寺文書}}』）には「野見社」の名で見えているので、当時{{jalink|国衙領|Q11421344|国衙領内}}に{{jalink|社領}}を有する神社であったろうとの指摘もある『新編一宮市史 本文編上』、一宮市、昭和52年。。なお、大字名の「宮後（みやうしろ）」は当神社の北方に位置する村落の意味であろうとの説がある（{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}『尾張国地名考』）。 === 文化財 === * {{jalink|スダジイ|Q846994}} - 目通り2.5m高20m、同種の中でも稀な大木でありながら樹勢は旺盛であり、一宮市の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}に指定されている。 *野見神社古墳 - 長径約20m、墳高約1mの小規模な{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}で、{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前Ⅲ期（5世紀前半）の築造と見られている。なお、当古墳が含まれる伊勢古墳群は市の{{jalink|史跡|Q1081138}}に指定されている。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}{{jalink|今伊勢駅|Q6002985}}より徒歩15分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告 第8巻東海道3』、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、平成元年 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|野見神社|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|lt=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6180005010054 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nomino Shrine|lt=Nomino Shrine|WD=Q135039467}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町宮後字稲荷1929', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:野見神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646128}} == Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 野見神社 |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町宮後|Q127432729}}字稲荷1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}}・{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月16日|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=野見神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''野見神社'''（のみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町|Q11376737}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}「野見神社」に比定され{{jalink|稲沢市|Q389562}}浅井の{{jalink|浅井神社 (稲沢市)||浅井神社}}に比定する説もある（{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}の『本国神名帳集説』や、それを受けた{{jalink|松平君山|Q6564909}}の『{{jalink|張州府志|Q137847623}}』（{{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}2年（1752年））、『{{jalink|尾張名所図絵||尾張名所図絵後編}}』（{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}12年（1842年））など）。、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}} * {{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} 上記3{{jalink|柱 (曖昧さ回避)|Q16283888|柱}}を{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100|主祭神}}に、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}と{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}する。 神社名の「野見」から、{{jalink|土師氏|Q10927758}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}である野見宿禰命、あるいは天穂日命と見るのが通説となっている。また、境内に{{jalink|今伊勢古墳群}}に含まれる'''野見神社古墳'''があり、{{jalink|勾玉|Q334232}}や{{jalink|埴輪|Q849376}}等が出土しているが、土師氏が{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}の製造のみならず、{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}の築造にも深く関わっていたことも参考になる。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明であるが、土師氏がこの地域を開拓したさいに創祀したと推測され、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では{{jalink|式内社#式内社の社格||国幣小社}}に列し、『国内神名帳|尾張国神名帳』では「従三位 野見天神」と記載されている。また、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}6年（{{jalink|1399年|Q6422}}）の「尾張国国衙正税未進注文」（『{{jalink|醍醐寺文書}}』）には「野見社」の名で見えているので、当時{{jalink|国衙領|Q11421344|国衙領内}}に{{jalink|社領}}を有する神社であったろうとの指摘もある『新編一宮市史 本文編上』、一宮市、昭和52年。。なお、大字名の「宮後（みやうしろ）」は当神社の北方に位置する村落の意味であろうとの説がある（{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}『尾張国地名考』）。 === 文化財 === * {{jalink|スダジイ|Q846994}} - 目通り2.5m高20m、同種の中でも稀な大木でありながら樹勢は旺盛であり、一宮市の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}に指定されている。 *野見神社古墳 - 長径約20m、墳高約1mの小規模な{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}で、{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前Ⅲ期（5世紀前半）の築造と見られている。なお、当古墳が含まれる伊勢古墳群は市の{{jalink|史跡|Q1081138}}に指定されている。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}{{jalink|今伊勢駅|Q6002985}}より徒歩15分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告 第8巻東海道3』、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、平成元年 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|野見神社|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 野見神社 |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町宮後|Q127432729}}字稲荷1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}}・{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月16日|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=野見神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''野見神社'''（のみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町|Q11376737}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}「野見神社」に比定され{{jalink|稲沢市|Q389562}}浅井の{{jalink|浅井神社 (稲沢市)||浅井神社}}に比定する説もある（{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}の『本国神名帳集説』や、それを受けた{{jalink|松平君山|Q6564909}}の『{{jalink|張州府志|Q137847623}}』（{{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}2年（1752年））、『{{jalink|尾張名所図絵||尾張名所図絵後編}}』（{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}12年（1842年））など）。、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}} * {{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} 上記3{{jalink|柱 (曖昧さ回避)|Q16283888|柱}}を{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100|主祭神}}に、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}と{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}する。 神社名の「野見」から、{{jalink|土師氏|Q10927758}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}である野見宿禰命、あるいは天穂日命と見るのが通説となっている。また、境内に{{jalink|今伊勢古墳群}}に含まれる'''野見神社古墳'''があり、{{jalink|勾玉|Q334232}}や{{jalink|埴輪|Q849376}}等が出土しているが、土師氏が{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}の製造のみならず、{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}の築造にも深く関わっていたことも参考になる。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明であるが、土師氏がこの地域を開拓したさいに創祀したと推測され、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では{{jalink|式内社#式内社の社格||国幣小社}}に列し、『国内神名帳|尾張国神名帳』では「従三位 野見天神」と記載されている。また、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}6年（{{jalink|1399年|Q6422}}）の「尾張国国衙正税未進注文」（『{{jalink|醍醐寺文書}}』）には「野見社」の名で見えているので、当時{{jalink|国衙領|Q11421344|国衙領内}}に{{jalink|社領}}を有する神社であったろうとの指摘もある『新編一宮市史 本文編上』、一宮市、昭和52年。。なお、大字名の「宮後（みやうしろ）」は当神社の北方に位置する村落の意味であろうとの説がある（{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}『尾張国地名考』）。 === 文化財 === * {{jalink|スダジイ|Q846994}} - 目通り2.5m高20m、同種の中でも稀な大木でありながら樹勢は旺盛であり、一宮市の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}に指定されている。 *野見神社古墳 - 長径約20m、墳高約1mの小規模な{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}で、{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前Ⅲ期（5世紀前半）の築造と見られている。なお、当古墳が含まれる伊勢古墳群は市の{{jalink|史跡|Q1081138}}に指定されている。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}{{jalink|今伊勢駅|Q6002985}}より徒歩15分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告 第8巻東海道3』、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、平成元年 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|野見神社|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町宮後字稲荷1929', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:野見神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646128}} == Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 野見神社 |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町宮後|Q127432729}}字稲荷1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}}・{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月16日|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=野見神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''野見神社'''（のみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町|Q11376737}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}「野見神社」に比定され{{jalink|稲沢市|Q389562}}浅井の{{jalink|浅井神社 (稲沢市)||浅井神社}}に比定する説もある（{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}の『本国神名帳集説』や、それを受けた{{jalink|松平君山|Q6564909}}の『{{jalink|張州府志|Q137847623}}』（{{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}2年（1752年））、『{{jalink|尾張名所図絵||尾張名所図絵後編}}』（{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}12年（1842年））など）。、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}} * {{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} 上記3{{jalink|柱 (曖昧さ回避)|Q16283888|柱}}を{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100|主祭神}}に、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}と{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}する。 神社名の「野見」から、{{jalink|土師氏|Q10927758}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}である野見宿禰命、あるいは天穂日命と見るのが通説となっている。また、境内に{{jalink|今伊勢古墳群}}に含まれる'''野見神社古墳'''があり、{{jalink|勾玉|Q334232}}や{{jalink|埴輪|Q849376}}等が出土しているが、土師氏が{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}の製造のみならず、{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}の築造にも深く関わっていたことも参考になる。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明であるが、土師氏がこの地域を開拓したさいに創祀したと推測され、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では{{jalink|式内社#式内社の社格||国幣小社}}に列し、『国内神名帳|尾張国神名帳』では「従三位 野見天神」と記載されている。また、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}6年（{{jalink|1399年|Q6422}}）の「尾張国国衙正税未進注文」（『{{jalink|醍醐寺文書}}』）には「野見社」の名で見えているので、当時{{jalink|国衙領|Q11421344|国衙領内}}に{{jalink|社領}}を有する神社であったろうとの指摘もある『新編一宮市史 本文編上』、一宮市、昭和52年。。なお、大字名の「宮後（みやうしろ）」は当神社の北方に位置する村落の意味であろうとの説がある（{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}『尾張国地名考』）。 === 文化財 === * {{jalink|スダジイ|Q846994}} - 目通り2.5m高20m、同種の中でも稀な大木でありながら樹勢は旺盛であり、一宮市の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}に指定されている。 *野見神社古墳 - 長径約20m、墳高約1mの小規模な{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}で、{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前Ⅲ期（5世紀前半）の築造と見られている。なお、当古墳が含まれる伊勢古墳群は市の{{jalink|史跡|Q1081138}}に指定されている。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}{{jalink|今伊勢駅|Q6002985}}より徒歩15分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告 第8巻東海道3』、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、平成元年 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|野見神社|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

{{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 野見神社 |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町宮後|Q127432729}}字稲荷1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}}・{{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}}・{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月16日|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=野見神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''野見神社'''（のみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|今伊勢町|Q11376737}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}「野見神社」に比定され{{jalink|稲沢市|Q389562}}浅井の{{jalink|浅井神社 (稲沢市)||浅井神社}}に比定する説もある（{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}の『本国神名帳集説』や、それを受けた{{jalink|松平君山|Q6564909}}の『{{jalink|張州府志|Q137847623}}』（{{jalink|宝暦|Q1194397}}2年（1752年））、『{{jalink|尾張名所図絵||尾張名所図絵後編}}』（{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}12年（1842年））など）。、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|野見宿禰|Q965742|野見宿禰命}} * {{jalink|アメノホヒ|Q10940685|天穂日命}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} 上記3{{jalink|柱 (曖昧さ回避)|Q16283888|柱}}を{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100|主祭神}}に、{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}と{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}}を{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}する。 神社名の「野見」から、{{jalink|土師氏|Q10927758}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}である野見宿禰命、あるいは天穂日命と見るのが通説となっている。また、境内に{{jalink|今伊勢古墳群}}に含まれる'''野見神社古墳'''があり、{{jalink|勾玉|Q334232}}や{{jalink|埴輪|Q849376}}等が出土しているが、土師氏が{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}の製造のみならず、{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}の築造にも深く関わっていたことも参考になる。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明であるが、土師氏がこの地域を開拓したさいに創祀したと推測され、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では{{jalink|式内社#式内社の社格||国幣小社}}に列し、『国内神名帳|尾張国神名帳』では「従三位 野見天神」と記載されている。また、{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}6年（{{jalink|1399年|Q6422}}）の「尾張国国衙正税未進注文」（『{{jalink|醍醐寺文書}}』）には「野見社」の名で見えているので、当時{{jalink|国衙領|Q11421344|国衙領内}}に{{jalink|社領}}を有する神社であったろうとの指摘もある『新編一宮市史 本文編上』、一宮市、昭和52年。。なお、大字名の「宮後（みやうしろ）」は当神社の北方に位置する村落の意味であろうとの説がある（{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}『尾張国地名考』）。 === 文化財 === * {{jalink|スダジイ|Q846994}} - 目通り2.5m高20m、同種の中でも稀な大木でありながら樹勢は旺盛であり、一宮市の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}に指定されている。 *野見神社古墳 - 長径約20m、墳高約1mの小規模な{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}で、{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前Ⅲ期（5世紀前半）の築造と見られている。なお、当古墳が含まれる伊勢古墳群は市の{{jalink|史跡|Q1081138}}に指定されている。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}{{jalink|今伊勢駅|Q6002985}}より徒歩15分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告 第8巻東海道3』、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、平成元年 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|野見神社|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

本文編上』、一宮市、昭和52年。。なお、大字名の「宮後（みやうしろ）」は当神社の北方に位置する村落の意味であろうとの説がある（{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}『尾張国地名考』）。 === 文化財 === * {{jalink|スダジイ|Q846994}} - 目通り2.5m高20m、同種の中でも稀な大木でありながら樹勢は旺盛であり、一宮市の{{jalink|天然記念物|Q23790}}に指定されている。 *野見神社古墳 - 長径約20m、墳高約1mの小規模な{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}で、{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}前Ⅲ期（5世紀前半）の築造と見られている。なお、当古墳が含まれる伊勢古墳群は市の{{jalink|史跡|Q1081138}}に指定されている。 === 交通 === *{{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}{{jalink|今伊勢駅|Q6002985}}より徒歩15分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 式内社研究會編『式内社調査報告 第8巻東海道3』、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、平成元年 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|野見神社|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická Owari|wuu|尾张国|zh_min_nan|Owari-kok|zh_yue|尾張國|zh|尾張國|WD=Q514506}} * {{ill|Q780420|lt=Q780420|WD=Q780420}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.315799|136.783011}} == image (P18) == * Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q965742|lt=Q965742|WD=Q965742}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181355 ** P3831: 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|lt=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6180005010054 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Nomino Shrine|lt=Nomino Shrine|WD=Q135039467}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町宮後字稲荷1929', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:野見神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646128}} == Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 16|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Imaise-chō|Q11376737}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{jalink|Asai Shrine (Inazawa)||Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{jalink|Inazawa|Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{jalink|Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623}}'' ({{jalink|1752|Q6679}}), and ''{{jalink|Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen||Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{jalink|1842|Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{jalink|pillars (deities)|Q16283888|deities}} are the main deities; {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{jalink|Imaise Kofun group}}, where {{jalink|magatama|Q334232}} and {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{jalink|pottery|Q1377111}} but also in building {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{jalink|Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{jalink|round tumulus|Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

{{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 16|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Imaise-chō|Q11376737}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{jalink|Asai Shrine (Inazawa)||Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{jalink|Inazawa|Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{jalink|Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623}}'' ({{jalink|1752|Q6679}}), and ''{{jalink|Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen||Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{jalink|1842|Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{jalink|pillars (deities)|Q16283888|deities}} are the main deities; {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{jalink|Imaise Kofun group}}, where {{jalink|magatama|Q334232}} and {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{jalink|pottery|Q1377111}} but also in building {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{jalink|Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{jalink|round tumulus|Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{jalink|Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{jalink|round tumulus|Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

{{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 16|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Imaise-chō|Q11376737}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{jalink|Asai Shrine (Inazawa)||Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{jalink|Inazawa|Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{jalink|Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623}}'' ({{jalink|1752|Q6679}}), and ''{{jalink|Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen||Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{jalink|1842|Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{jalink|pillars (deities)|Q16283888|deities}} are the main deities; {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{jalink|Imaise Kofun group}}, where {{jalink|magatama|Q334232}} and {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{jalink|pottery|Q1377111}} but also in building {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{jalink|Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{jalink|round tumulus|Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

{{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 16|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Imaise-chō|Q11376737}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{jalink|Asai Shrine (Inazawa)||Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{jalink|Inazawa|Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{jalink|Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623}}'' ({{jalink|1752|Q6679}}), and ''{{jalink|Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen||Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{jalink|1842|Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{jalink|pillars (deities)|Q16283888|deities}} are the main deities; {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{jalink|Imaise Kofun group}}, where {{jalink|magatama|Q334232}} and {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{jalink|pottery|Q1377111}} but also in building {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{jalink|Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{jalink|round tumulus|Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}

In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{jalink|Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{jalink|round tumulus|Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]]

{{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 16|Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Imaise-chō|Q11376737}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{jalink|Asai Shrine (Inazawa)||Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{jalink|Inazawa|Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{jalink|Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623}}'' ({{jalink|1752|Q6679}}), and ''{{jalink|Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen||Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{jalink|1842|Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{jalink|pillars (deities)|Q16283888|deities}} are the main deities; {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{jalink|Haji clan|Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{jalink|Imaise Kofun group}}, where {{jalink|magatama|Q334232}} and {{jalink|haniwa|Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{jalink|pottery|Q1377111}} but also in building {{jalink|kofun|Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{jalink|kokuhei shōsha||kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{jalink|Ōei|Q1193078}} ({{jalink|1399|Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{jalink|Daigoji documents}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{jalink|shrine lands}} within the {{jalink|kokuga domain|Q11421344|kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{jalink|Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{jalink|round tumulus|Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{jalink|historic site|Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Imaise Station|Q6002985}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} of {{jalink|Nagoya Railroad|Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{jalink|Kokugakuin University||Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{jalink|Nomi Shrine|Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha||WD=|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill||lt=Daigoji documents|UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill||lt=shrine lands|UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University||WD=|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)||WD=|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen||WD=|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha||WD=|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University||WD=|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)||WD=|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen||WD=|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha||WD=|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University||WD=|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|lt=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町宮後字稲荷1929', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:野見神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646128}} == Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
