{{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町宮後字稲荷1929', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:野見神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646128}} == Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562|1=Inazawa}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754|1=Charm quark}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities|1=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729|1=Imaise-chō Miyaushiro}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}}・{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737|1=Imaise}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562|1=Inazawa}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754|1=Charm quark}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities|1=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729|1=Imaise-chō Miyaushiro}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}}・{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737|1=Imaise}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562|1=Inazawa}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754|1=Charm quark}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities|1=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562|1=Inazawa}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754|1=Charm quark}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities|1=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)}}[[Category:qq]]

Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729|1=Imaise-chō Miyaushiro}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}}・{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737|1=Imaise}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562|1=Inazawa}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754|1=Charm quark}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities|1=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:qq]]

In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:qq]]

Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729|1=Imaise-chō Miyaushiro}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}}・{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737|1=Imaise}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562|1=Inazawa}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754|1=Charm quark}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities|1=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:qq]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729|1=Imaise-chō Miyaushiro}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}}・{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737|1=Imaise}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562|1=Inazawa}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754|1=Charm quark}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities|1=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]]

Japanese content == {{ページ番号|date=2014年10月27日 (月) 12:53 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = Nomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nomi-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyago, Imaise-chō|Q127432729|WD=Q127432729|1=Imaise-chō Miyaushiro}} Aza Inari 1929 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}}・{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}・{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Nomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Imaise-chō|Q11376737|WD=Q11376737|1=Imaise}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Nomi Shrine” of Nakashima District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha list|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}There is also a theory identifying it with {{ill|lt=Asai Shrine (Inazawa)|UNKNOWN|lt=Asai Shrine}} in Asai, {{ill|lt=Inazawa|Q389562|WD=Q389562|1=Inazawa}} (e.g., Amano Nobukage’s ''Hongoku Jinmyōchō Shūsetsu'', Matsudaira Kunizan’s ''{{ill|lt=Chōshū Fushi|Q137847623|WD=Q137847623|1=Chōshūfushi}}'' ({{ill|lt=1752|Q6679|WD=Q6679|1=1492}}), and ''{{ill|lt=Owari Meisho Zue Kōhen|UNKNOWN|lt=Owari Meisho Zue, latter volume}}'' ({{ill|lt=1842|Q6754|WD=Q6754|1=Charm quark}})).. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} * {{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} These three {{ill|lt=pillars (deities)|Q16283888|WD=Q16283888|lt=deities|1=deities}} are the main deities; {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susa-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} are additionally enshrined. From the shrine name “Nomi,” the common view is that the deity is either Nomi no Sukune-no-Mikoto (ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Haji clan|Q10927758|WD=Q10927758|1=Haji clan}}) or Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto. In the precinct is the '''Nomi Shrine Kofun''', part of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imaise Kofun group|1=UNKNOWN}}, where {{ill|lt=magatama|Q334232|WD=Q334232|1=Magatama}} and {{ill|lt=haniwa|Q849376|WD=Q849376|1=Haniwa}} have been unearthed. This also supports that the Haji clan was deeply involved not only in producing {{ill|lt=pottery|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} but also in building {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}. === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is presumed to have been established when the Haji clan developed this region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a {{ill|lt=kokuhei shōsha|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei small shrine}}, and in the ''Owari-koku Jinmyōchō'' it is recorded as “Junior Third Rank, Nomi Tenjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}), the “Owari Province kokuga unpaid-tax report” (in the ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daigoji documents|1=UNKNOWN}}'') mentions “Nomi-sha,” leading to the view that it held {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine lands|1=UNKNOWN}} within the {{ill|lt=kokuga domain|Q11421344|WD=Q11421344|lt=kokuga territory|1=Kokugaryō}} at the time''Newly Compiled History of Ichinomiya City, Main Text Upper Volume'', Ichinomiya City, 1977.. There is also a theory that the district name “Miyaushiro” means a settlement north of this shrine (Tsuda Masao, ''Study of Owari Place Names''). === Cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Castanopsis sieboldii|Q846994|WD=Q846994|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} - Trunk circumference 2.5 m and height 20 m; a rare giant of its species with vigorous growth, designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Ichinomiya. *Nomi Shrine Kofun - A small {{ill|lt=round tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} about 20 m long and 1 m high, thought to date to early period III of the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} (first half of the 5th century). The Imaise Kofun group containing this tumulus is designated a city {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}. === Access === *15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Imaise Station|Q6002985|WD=Q6002985|1=Imaise Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha, Vol. 8 Tōkaidō 3'', {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press}}, 1989. === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nomi Shrine|Q11646127|WD=Q11646127|1=Nomii Shrine (Takatsuki)}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646128)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]
